# quantlaw

This package contains coding utilities for quantitative legal studies.

## Modules

The package currently consists of two modules.

### de_extract

`quantlaw.de_extract` is an extractor for references to statutes in German legal texts.
In contrast to most other named entity recognition packages, this module not only
identifies the references but also extracts their content. This can, e.g., be used to
quantitatively analyze the structure of the law.

For example, we can extract the content of two references in the following text.

Source text:

"In den Fällen des **§ 111d Absatz 1 Satz 2** der *Strafprozessordnung* findet **§ 91**
der *Insolvenzordnung* keine Anwendung."

The extracted data would be:

1. `[[['§', '111d'], ['Abs', '1'], ['Satz', '2']]]` for the law `StPO`
2. `[[['§', '91']]]` for the law `InsO`

Getting started in the documentation contains a minimal example.

### utils

`quantlaw.utils` contains several utilities that are helpful to analyze the structure of
the law with `BeautifulSoup` and `networkx`. The documentation contains further
information about the individual usages.

## Installation

Python 3.7.9 is recommended. Our package is provided via `pip install quantlaw`.

## Related Projects and Publications

It is, inter alia, used to produce the results reported in the following publications:

- Daniel Martin Katz, Corinna Coupette, Janis Beckedorf, and Dirk Hartung, Complex Societies and the Growth of the Law, *Sci. Rep.* **10** (2020), [https://doi.org/10.1038/s41598-020-73623-x](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41598-020-73623-x)
- Corinna Coupette, Janis Beckedorf, Dirk Hartung, Michael Bommarito, and Daniel Martin Katz, Measuring Law Over Time, *Front. Phys.* **9:658463** (2021), https://doi.org/10.3389/fphy.2021.658463
- Corinna Coupette, and Dirk Hartung, Rechtsstrukturvergleichung, *RabelsZ* **86**, 935-975 (2022), https://doi.org/10.1628/rabelsz-2022-0082
- Corinna Coupette, Dirk Hartung, Janis Beckedorf, Maximilian Böther, and Daniel Martin Katz, Law Smells, *Artif Intell Law* **31**, 335-368 (2023), https://doi.org/10.1007/s10506-022-09315-w
- Janis Beckedorf, Komplexität des Rechts, Mohr Siebeck, to appear (2025), https://doi.org/10.1628/978-3-16-164476-4

Related Repositories:
- [Complex Societies and the Growth of the Law](https://github.com/QuantLaw/Complex-Societies-and-Growth) ([Publication Release](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.4070769))
- [Measuring Law Over Time](https://github.com/QuantLaw/Measuring-Law-Over-Time) ([Publication Release](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.4660191))
- [Law Smells](https://github.com/QuantLaw/law-smells) ([Publication Release](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.6468193))
- [Komplexität des Rechts](https://github.com/beckedorf/komplexitaet-des-rechts) ([Publication Release](https://doi.org/10.1628/978-3-16-164476-4-appendix))
- [Legal Data Preprocessing](https://github.com/QuantLaw/legal-data-preprocessing) ([Latest Publication Release](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.4070772))
- [Legal Data Clustering](https://github.com/QuantLaw/legal-data-clustering) ([Latest Publication Release](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.4070774))

Related Data: 
- [Preprocessed Input Data for *Sci. Rep.* **10** (2020)](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.4070767)
- [Preprocessed Input Data for *Measuring Law Over Time*, *Front. Phys.* **9:658463** (2021)](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.4660133)
- [Preprocessed Input Data for *Komplexität des Rechts*, Mohr Siebeck, to appear (2025)](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.14298980)
- [Preprocessed Input Data for *Law Smells*, *Artif Intell Law* **31**, 335–368 (2023)](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.6468191)

## Collaboration

Please format the code using `isort`, `black`, and `flake8`. A convenient option to
ensure correct formatting of the code is to
[pip install pre-commit](https://pypi.org/project/pre-commit/) and run
`pre-commit install` to add code checking and reformatting as git pre-commit hook.

functions.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_parse_patterns.generate_sgb_dict
documentation:
Returns a dictionary, Its keys are different ways how SGB books are cited. They are
mapped to values that represent the keys to the SGB books.

classes.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_areas.StatutesExtractor

methods.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_areas.StatutesExtractor.search
documentation:
Finds the next occurrence of a statute reference in a given text

Args:
    text: The text to search in.
    pos: Position to start searching.

Returns: The match or None if no references are found.

methods.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_areas.StatutesExtractor.find_all
documentation: Like search but returns a generator of all matches found in text

methods.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_areas.StatutesExtractor.get_suffix_and_law_name
documentation:
Returns: A tuple containing length of

    1. the article between numbers and law name (eg. " der ")
    2. length of name of law as in the given string
    3. The type of the reference.

    If not found lengths are 0.

methods.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_areas.StatutesExtractor.get_dict_law_name_len
documentation:
Determines if the test_str starts with a law name given with self.laws_lookup.

Returns: The length matched law name or 0.

methods.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_areas.StatutesExtractor.get_no_suffix_ignore_law_name_len
documentation:
Returns: Length of the law name in chars, if no suffix is present that connects
    the main area with the law name or 0 if no law name of this type was found

methods.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_areas.StatutesExtractor.get_sgb_law_name_len
documentation:
Returns: The length of the SGB law name in chars or 0 if no law name of this
    type was found

methods.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_areas.StatutesExtractor.get_eu_law_name_len
documentation:
Returns: The length of the law name of european legislation in chars or
    0 if no law name of this type was found

methods.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_areas.StatutesExtractor.get_ignore_law_name_len
documentation:
Returns: Th length of a law name to ignore in chars or 0 if no law name of
    this type was found
Class to find areas of citations to German statutes and regulations

classes.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_abstract.StatusMatch

methods.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_abstract.StatusMatch.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    text: The text the match was found in.
    start: The position in the text where the trigger/main area starts
    end: The position where the trigger/main area ends.

methods.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_abstract.StatusMatch.has_main_area
documentation:
Returns True if the match has a main area and thus its content can be parsed by
StatutesParser

Base class to report the areas of citations to German statutes and regulations
(also if a trigger e.g. '§' is found but it is not followed by a citation).

classes.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_abstract.StatutesMatchWithMainArea

methods.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_abstract.StatutesMatchWithMainArea.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    suffix_len: Length of the suffix that may connect the main area of the
        citation to the name of the referenced law
    law_len: Length of the name of the referenced law
    law_match_type: Typ of the reference that depends on the law name.
        E.g. dict, internal, ignore, eu, sgb
    *args:
    **kwargs:

methods.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_abstract.StatutesMatchWithMainArea.main_text
documentation:
Returns: The main area of a citation, which is the text part that specifies
the cited part of the statute but omitting the name of the law.
E.g. "§ 123 Abs. 4, Nr 5 und 6"

methods.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_abstract.StatutesMatchWithMainArea.suffix_text
documentation:
Returns: The text joins the main part of the citation with the name of the
cited law.
An empty sting is return if no law is specified. This is typically the case
for references within a law.

methods.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_abstract.StatutesMatchWithMainArea.law_text
documentation: Returns: The referenced law.

Class to report the areas of citations to German statutes and regulations where a
main area is found after the trigger "§ 123" where "123" is the main area.

classes.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_abstract.StatutesProcessor

methods.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_abstract.StatutesProcessor.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    laws_lookup: See laws_lookup property for details.

methods.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_abstract.StatutesProcessor.laws_lookup
documentation:
A dictionary to find of the law names to extract.
Keys are names of laws that are used in the source text used to cite
laws. Values are unique identifiers of laws.
For optimal results is is recommended to make the list a exhaustive as
possible to reduce the chance that references are false treated as
internal references within a law because the name of the referenced law
is not recognized. The names of the laws should be provided in a
stemmed format using the stemmer provided in
`quantlaw.de_extract.stemming.stem_law_name`.

methods.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_abstract.StatutesProcessor.match_law_name
documentation:
Checks if the text begins with a law name provided in self.laws_lookup_keys.

Returns: The matched substring.

Abstract class to extract and parse statute references.
The abstract class provides the names of laws they are cited with.

classes.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_parse.StringCaseException
documentation:
Exception is raised if a unit in a reference cannot be parsed. In this case it is
often an issue of upper oder lower case formatting.

classes.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_parse.NoUnitMatched
documentation: Exception is raised if a unit in a refren cannot be parsed.

classes.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_parse.StatutesParser

methods.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_parse.StatutesParser.parse_main
documentation:
Parses a string containing a reference to a specific section within a given law.
E.g. "§ 123 Abs. 4 Satz 5 und 6".
The parsed informtaion is formatted into lists nested in lists nested in lists.

The outer list is a list of references.

References are lists of path components. A path component is e.g. "Abs. 4".

A path component is represented by a list with two elements: The first
contains the unit the second the value.

The example above would be represented as
`[[['§', '123'], ['Abs', '4'], ['Satz', '5']],
[['§', '123'], ['Abs', '4'], ['Satz', '6']]]`.

Args:
    main_text: string to parse

Returns: The parsed reference.

methods.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_parse.StatutesParser.parse_law
documentation:
Parses the law information from a references found by StatutesMatchWithMainArea

Args:
    main_text: E.g. "§ 123 Abs. 4 und 5 Nr. 6"
    law_text: E.g. "BGB"
    match_type: E.g. "dict"

Returns: The key of a parse law.

methods.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_parse.StatutesParser.stem_unit
documentation:
Brings a unit into a standard format. E.g. removes abbreviations, grammatical
differences spelling errors, etc.

Args:
    unit: A string containing a unit that should be converted into a standard
        format.

Returns: Unit in a standard format as string. E.g. §, Art, Nr, Halbsatz,
    Anhang, ...

methods.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_parse.StatutesParser.is_unit
documentation: Returns: True if the token is a unit

methods.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_parse.StatutesParser.is_pre_numb
documentation:
Returns: True if the token is a number that comes *before* the unit.
E.g. '*erster* Halbsatz'

methods.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_parse.StatutesParser.is_numb
documentation: Returns: True if the token is a 'numeric' value of the reference.

methods.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_parse.StatutesParser.fix_errors_in_citation
documentation: Fix some common inconsistencies in the references such as double spaces.

methods.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_parse.StatutesParser.split_citation_into_enum_parts
documentation:
A citation can contain references to multiple parts of the law.
E.g. '§§ 20 und 35' or 'Art. 3 Abs. 1 Satz 1, Abs. 3 Satz 1'.
The citation is split into parts so that each referenced section of the law is
separated. E.g. '§§ 20' and '35' resp. 'Art. 3 Abs. 1 Satz 1' and
'Abs. 3 Satz 1'.
However, ranges are not spit: E.g. "§§ 1 bis 10" will not be split.

methods.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_parse.StatutesParser.split_parts_accidently_joined
documentation:
Reformats the parsed references to separate accitently joined references.
E.g. the original referehence "§ 123 § 126" will not be split by
split_citation_into_enum_parts because the separation is falsly not indicated by
a ',', 'or' etc. It come from the unit '§' that it can be inferred that the
citation contains references to two parts of statutes.
This function accounts for the case that the unit '§' or 'Art' appears twice in
the same reference path and split the path into several elements.

methods.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_parse.StatutesParser.infer_units
documentation:
In some cases of an enumeration a numeric value is not directed prefixed by
the corresponding unit. E.g. "§ 123 Abs. 1 S. 2, 3 S. 4". In this case "3"
is not prefixed with its unit. Instead it can be inferred by looking at the
whole citation that it is next higher unit of "S.", hence "Abs.". These
inferred units are added to parsed data.

methods.name: quantlaw.de_extract.statutes_parse.StatutesParser.split_citation_part
documentation:
A string a tokenizes. Tokens are identified as units or values. Pairs are
built to connect the units with their respective values. If the unit cannot
be indentified (and must be inferred later) None is returned.

Args:
    string: A string that is part of a reference and cites *one* part a statute.

Retruns: As a generator tuples are returned, each containing the unit (or None)
    and the respecive value.
Class to parse the content of a reference area identified by StatutesExtractor

documentation:
Create custom induced subgraph.

Args:
    filter_type: 'node' or 'edge'
    filter_attribute: attribute to filter on
    filter_values: attribute values to evaluate to `True`

functions.name: quantlaw.utils.networkx.hierarchy_graph
documentation:
Remove reference edges from G.
Wrapper around induced_subgraph.

functions.name: quantlaw.utils.networkx.multi_to_weighted
documentation: Converts a multidigraph into a weighted digraph.

functions.name: quantlaw.utils.networkx.get_leaves
documentation:
Args:
    G: A tree as directed graph with edges from root to leaves

Returns: Set of leaves of the tree G

functions.name: quantlaw.utils.networkx.decay_function
documentation: Returns a decay function to create a weighted sequence graph.

functions.name: quantlaw.utils.networkx.sequence_graph
documentation:
Creates sequence graph for G, consisting of seqitems and their cross-references
only,
where neighboring seqitems are connected via edges in both directions.

Args:
    seq_decay_func: function to calculate sequence edge weight based on distance
        between neighboring nodes
    seq_ref_ratio: ratio between a sequence edge weight when nodes in the
        sequence are at minimum distance from each other and a reference edge weight

functions.name: quantlaw.utils.networkx.get_new_edges
documentation: Convenience function to avoid list comprehension over four lines.

functions.name: quantlaw.utils.networkx.quotient_graph
documentation:
Generate the quotient graph with all nodes sharing the same node_attribute condensed
into a single node. Simplest use case is aggregation by law_name.

functions.name: quantlaw.utils.networkx.aggregate_attr_in_quotient_graph
documentation:
Sums attributes of nodes in an original graph per community and adds the sum to the
nodes in a quotient graph.

Args:
    nG: Quotient graph
    G: Original graph
        new_nodes: Mapping of nodes in the quotient graph to an iterable of nodes in
        the original graph that are represented by the node in the quotient graph.
    aggregation_attrs: attributes to aggregate

functions.name: quantlaw.utils.networkx.load_graph_from_csv_files
documentation:
Loads a networkx MultiDiGraph from a nodelist and edgelist
formatted as .csv.gz files.
The node csv must have a 'key' column that serves as a node key.
Other columns are added as node attributes.
The edge csv must have a the columns 'u', 'v', 'edge_type'.
If filter is node all nodes will be loaded.
By default subeqitems will be excluded.
If filter is a callable, it is called with a pandas.DataFrame loaded
from the csv as the only argument.
The callable must return values to filter the DataFrame.

Args:
    crossreference_folder: Folder containing the edgelists
    file_basename: base filename of the edgelists
        (will be suffixed with '.nodes.csv.gz' and '.edges.csv.gz')
    filter: Filters the nodes to load. Options "exclude_subseqitems",
        None or a function that filters a pandas.DataFrame
    filter_by_edge_types: Filters the edges to load. None in includes all
        edges. You can also provide a list of edge_types.
        E.g. `['containment', 'reference']`.
